evals.prompt: I want to wire attested quality gates for my repo. It is a HYPOTHETICAL public repo called `examplecorp/widget` (owner/org `examplecorp`), language TypeScript/JavaScript, default branch `main`, no container image and no running app. Do NOT call the GitHub API or git — treat this as a paper exercise. Generate the Phase 2 thin-caller workflow that fans out the universal merge-time gates: SAST, SCA, supply-chain posture, Trivy IaC+license, and pin-check. Because you cannot resolve the central repo's real commit SHA offline, pin every central reusable `uses:` to the placeholder 40-hex SHA `1111111111111111111111111111111111111111`. Write the finished workflow to `quality-gates.yml` in your outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: A .github/workflows-style caller named quality-gates.yml that calls examplecorp/.github/.github/workflows/{reusable-sast-codeql,reusable-sca-osv,reusable-scorecard,reusable-trivy,pin-check}.yml, each pinned to a 40-char SHA, with least-privilege per-job permissions and the __org__ placeholder replaced by examplecorp.

deterministic_checks.description: central-repo path uses the real org, not a placeholder
central reusables are pinned to a 40-char SHA

expectations: Every `uses:` of a central reusable is pinned to the 40-char SHA placeholder, and none is pinned to a moving tag (no `@v1`, `@main`, `@master`).

Each gate job declares least-privilege permissions appropriate to its work (e.g. `security-events: write` on the SAST/Trivy jobs that upload SARIF), rather than granting broad write at the workflow level.

evals.prompt: Audit the action-pinning posture of an existing workflow against the gh-attested hard rules. This is a HYPOTHETICAL repo `examplecorp/widget`; do NOT call the GitHub API. Use only the file content provided below. List every unsafely pinned `uses:` and state the correct remediation. Write a markdown report to `pin-audit.md` in your outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: A pin-audit.md report that flags all three tag-pinned actions (actions/checkout, aquasecurity/trivy-action, github/codeql-action) as unsafe, prescribes full 40-char commit-SHA pinning, and cites the trivy-action supply-chain compromise as the cautionary precedent.

files.content:
name: ci
on: [push]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.20.0
      - uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3

expectations: The report flags all three `uses:` as unsafely tag-pinned (not pinned to a commit SHA) and recommends replacing each tag with a full 40-character commit SHA.
It cites the aquasecurity/trivy-action force-push/malware compromise (CVE-2026-33634) as the standing reason tags are unsafe.

evals.prompt: A downstream consumer wants to independently verify the SAST gate attestation on a published image. The subject is `oci://ghcr.io/examplecorp/app@sha256:abc123` and the owner is `examplecorp`. This is HYPOTHETICAL — do NOT actually run gh or call any API. Produce the exact `gh attestation verify` command they should run, correct under this org's SLSA L3 attested-delivery model. Write the command (a short shell snippet) to `verify.sh` in your outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: A verify.sh containing a `gh attestation verify` invocation pinned with --owner examplecorp AND --signer-workflow pointing at the central seam workflow reusable-attest-scan.yml, the SAST predicate-type URI, and NO cosign-only flags.

deterministic_checks.description: pins the signer workflow (mandatory under SLSA L3)
signer is the central seam workflow
does NOT use the cosign-only flag gh rejects
does NOT use the cosign-only flag gh rejects

expectations: The command pins `--signer-workflow` to the central seam workflow (`reusable-attest-scan.yml`), correctly reflecting that under SLSA L3 the Fulcio SAN is the central signer rather than the source repository — `--owner` alone would be insufficient.

evals.prompt: On my HYPOTHETICAL repo `examplecorp/widget` I want to enable org-tier gitleaks, which needs a `GITLEAKS_LICENSE` secret. Tell me how to set it up. Do NOT call the GitHub API. Write your setup instructions to `secret-setup.md` in your outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: A secret-setup.md that instructs the user to run `gh secret set GITLEAKS_LICENSE` themselves, never printing or fabricating the value, consistent with the skill's never-read/write/log-a-secret contract.

deterministic_checks.description: emits the correct user-run command by name

expectations: The instructions tell the user to run the `gh secret set` command themselves so the value is piped from their own input; the skill emits only the secret NAME and the command and never fabricates, inlines a real value (e.g. via `--body "<value>"`), requests the value in chat, or prints it. Referencing `--body` solely as a pattern to AVOID is correct behavior, not a violation.
The guidance reflects the skill's never-read/write/commit/log-a-secret contract — most gates need no secret at all and GITLEAKS_LICENSE is set by the user out-of-band.

evals.prompt: I already gathered the state of my HYPOTHETICAL repo `examplecorp/widget` (see the attached files). Do NOT call the GitHub API — use ONLY the provided state. Produce the Phase 0 coverage matrix across all 12 quality gates (present? attested? merge gate? deploy gate?) and a list of in-scope gaps. Write it as a markdown table to `coverage-matrix.md` in your outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: A coverage-matrix.md markdown table with one row per each of the 12 gates, correctly marking SAST present (a codeql.yml exists) and SCA/posture/Trivy/VEX/SBOM as gaps, with k6 and DAST out-of-scope (no running app/container).

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import sys; rows=[l for l in open('coverage-matrix.md') if l.lstrip().startswith('|')]; sys.exit(0 if len(rows)>=13 else 1)"
deterministic_checks.description: matrix is a table with at least 12 gate rows (plus header/separator)

expectations: The matrix marks SAST (CodeQL) as present because a `codeql.yml` workflow exists, and marks SCA, supply-chain posture, Trivy (IaC/license), VEX and SBOM as gaps not yet covered.
k6 (load) and DAST (ZAP) are marked out-of-scope / documented-not-wired because the repo has no running app and no container image.

evals.prompt: Wire ALL gates — including load testing (k6) and DAST (ZAP) — for my HYPOTHETICAL repo `examplecorp/pylib` (owner `examplecorp`). It is a pure Python library: no running service, no container image, no load script, no target URL. Do NOT call the GitHub API. Pin central reusables to the placeholder 40-hex SHA `1111111111111111111111111111111111111111`. Write the caller to `quality-gates.yml` and a short note to `scope-notes.md` explaining what you wired versus what you deliberately did not, in your outputs directory.
evals.expected_output: A quality-gates.yml that actively wires the gates that apply to a library (SAST, SCA, posture, pin-check) but leaves k6 and ZAP unwired (commented/omitted), plus a scope-notes.md explaining that k6/ZAP are opt-in and out-of-scope without a running target.

deterministic_checks.description: SAST still wired for a library

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import sys; bad=[l for l in open('quality-gates.yml') if 'reusable-k6.yml' in l and not l.lstrip().startswith('#')]; sys.exit(1 if bad else 0)"
deterministic_checks.description: k6 is NOT actively wired (any reusable-k6 line is commented)

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import sys; bad=[l for l in open('quality-gates.yml') if 'reusable-zap.yml' in l and not l.lstrip().startswith('#')]; sys.exit(1 if bad else 0)"
deterministic_checks.description: ZAP is NOT actively wired (any reusable-zap line is commented)

expectations: The skill declines to actively wire k6 and ZAP for a target with no running app/container, marking them documented-not-wired rather than fabricating a load script or target URL.
The library-applicable gates (SAST, SCA, posture, pin-check) ARE wired in the caller.
